title: lotus eriophorus
intro: Lotus eriophorus, also known as the Indian lotus, is a floating aquatic plant native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia. Its rhizomes have been used in traditional medicine for centuries, exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. Consuming Lotus eriophorus is said to provide several health benefits, including the reduction of fever, relief from digestive issues, and the alleviation of respiratory problems. The plant is commonly prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in traditional Ayurvedic and Unani medicine practices.
